question_text: A photocopier uses static electricity to transfer toner powder onto paper. Why is static electricity (rather than a liquid ink) useful for this application?
correct_answer: Static charge attracts the toner powder precisely / The charged toner follows the pattern on the drum / This allows detailed, accurate copying without liquid mess / No drying time needed
explanation: Static electricity allows toner powder to be attracted to specific areas of the photocopier drum in the exact pattern needed, creating a precise copy without the mess or drying time of liquid ink.
